title: Angkor Wat Stories in the Bas-Reliefs: The Churning of the Ocean of Milk and Depictions of Devis - Angkor Grace
highlights: Stories, One of the most fascinating and symbolic stories depicted in the bas-reliefs of Angkor Wat is the Churning of the Ocean of Milk. This story, drawn from Hindu mythology, is a cosmic tale of the gods (Devas) and demons (Asuras) who, in a fierce battle for immortality, churn the ocean to extract the amrita—the elixir of life. In the Angkor Wat bas-reliefs, this scene comes to life with great detail. The gods and demons are shown pulling a massive serpent, Vasuki, wrapped around Mount Mandara, using it as a rope to churn the ocean. As the churning  ...    In addition to the Churning of the Ocean of Milk, the bas-reliefs of Angkor Wat are rich with portrayals of divine female figures or Devis. These goddesses are central to the spiritual and artistic themes of the Khmer Empire, often  ...    Lakshmi, as mentioned, is prominently featured, symbolizing wealth, beauty, and abundance.
